Step1: Candidates shall register himself / herself on the university Website www.iub.edu.pk by filling the registration form.
Step 2: After completing registration he / she will be able to fill Admission application form.
Step 3: After submission of valid application form the system will generate a challan form, to be printed by the candidate.
Step 4: The applicant shall deposit prescribed admission processing fee on auto generated challan in any nearest HBL branch.

Step 5: If applicant wants to apply in another program / department, he / she will go back to step 2. For every application form candidate will get a new challan and he / she will have to deposit separate application processing fee.
Step 6: If an applicant needs to update his / her information, he / she will login using candidate registration information and password.
Step 7: The Candidate will be able to check the status of his / her application online.

Step 8: If the candidate’s name appears in a merit list, he/she will present himself/herself in person, with in due time, along with all original documents to the admission committee of the respective department and follow the instructions of the committee.

Undergraduate Need Based Scholarship: 50% tuition waiver for eligible students with 75% intermediate/IBCC marks, financial need, and CGPA ≥3.5; reduced to 25% for CGPA 3.49‑3.0; deadline July 15.

Undergraduate Major Merit Scholarship: 50% tuition waiver for students with 85% marks (or 3 A’s) and CGPA ≥3.75; 25% waiver for 80% marks and CGPA ≥3.5; scholarship maintained each term with CGPA thresholds.
Undergraduate Minor Merit Scholarship (MMS I): fee waiver for a 2‑credit course for 1st‑year students with GPA 3.5; not combinable with other scholarships.
Undergraduate Minor Merit Scholarship (MMS II): fee waiver for a 4‑credit course for 2nd‑4th year students with GPA 4.0; can be combined with Major Merit and Need Based scholarships.
Undergraduate Student TA Ship: tuition credit for a 4‑credit course for 2nd‑4th year students with CGPA ≥3.75; additive to other scholarships.
Graduate Need Based Scholarship (MBA): 50% tuition waiver for students with CGPA ≥3.5 in 4‑year Bachelors and prior need‑based scholarship; reduced to 25% for CGPA 3.49‑3.0; deadline July 15.
Graduate Major Merit Scholarship (2‑year MBA): 50% tuition waiver for BBA/BSc (Hons) graduates with CGPA ≥3.75; reduced to 25% for CGPA 3.74‑3.5; maintained each term.
Graduate Major Merit Scholarship (1.5‑year MBA): 50% tuition waiver for graduates with CGPA ≥3.75; 25% waiver for CGPA ≥3.5; same CGPA thresholds as 2‑year program.
Graduate Minor Merit Scholarship (MBA): fee waiver for a 3‑credit course for MBA students with CGPA ≥3.75; additive to major merit and need‑based scholarships.
Graduate Student TA Ship (MBA): tuition credit for a 3‑credit course for MBA students not on full scholarship with CGPA ≥3.5 (or 2nd‑year students with CGPA ≥3.5 after first year).
Mahmood Chaudhry Scholarship: 100% tuition scholarship for one 1.5‑year MBA student already on need‑based scholarship who attains the highest CGPA in the first term; awarded for the remaining year.
Executive MBA Major Merit Scholarship: 50% tuition waiver for eligible BBA/BSc (Hons) graduates with CGPA ≥3.75; same reduction rules as other MBA merit scholarships.
Post‑Graduate PhD Merit Scholarship (Economics & Business Administration): 100% tuition waiver for students with CGPA ≥3.0 after MPhil/MS; maintained each term.

Scholarship_notes.val: In Pakistan, currently, less than 8% of the 17-23 age group is enrolled in any form of higher education that is amongst the lowest in the world. The estimated population of this age group will be 30 million by the year 2015. Needs based scholarships would be an answer to offset the challenge of financial constraints. Although in the majority of public sector universities the tuition fee is subsidized but it is still unaffordable for most of the students. Since 35 percent of the Pakistani population is living below the poverty line, it is almost impossible get the higher education for this segment of the society. The objective is to provide equal opportunity for the talented students who are at disadvantage being incapable of meeting cost of education at leading higher education institutions. The Student Financial Aid Office at University of Peshawar is here to serve student body and provide financial assistance in the fairest and most efficient way possible. The mission of the Students Financial Aid Office is to provide University Students with the timely delivery of financial assistance while maintaining accountability and proper stewardship of the public, institutional and private funds with which it is entrusted. We are committed to provide courteous service to support the academic mission and goals of the University and its students. This office provides students with an abundance of general financial aid information to assist students in understanding processes and in making informed financial decisions. Prospective students and their parents are especially invited to visit for general information about university costs, the types of financial aid that are available, the financial aid process, and necessary financial aid application requirements to understand eligibility of the students for financial aid. Prime Minister's National ICT Scholarship Program 2014 at Undergraduate Level for I.T and C.S. Ministry of Information Technology announces this wonderful opportunity for the talented students of rural / nonmetropolitan areas of Pakistan to study in top ICT universities of the country. Applicants have either passed or appeared in F.Sc Examination with Mathematics and Physics in 2013 or 2014 as a regular student from a school / college registered with National ICT R&D Fund. Candidates must have obtained 60% or above marks in Matriculation (Science Group) exam. Ehsaas Undergraduate Scholarship Program aims to eliminate unequal access. Prime Minister's Tuition Fee Payment Scheme for Masters / M.S / M.Phil and Ph. D Students of Less Developed Areas pays tuition for students from less developed areas. HEC-Needs Based Scholarship awarded 250 scholarships for the session 2013-14. Prime Minister's National Programme for Provision of Laptops to Talented Students distributes 100,000 laptops. HEC-French Needs Based Scholarship for 6 Pakistani Universities. USAID Scholarship For B.Ed (Hons) Pre-Step provides 15 scholarships. UoP Merit Cum Need Based Scholarship Program awarded to 200 students in session 2013-14. University of Peshawar Merit Scholarships for B. A/B.Sc Position Holders award full fee waiver. University of Peshawar Brother/Sister Concession offers half tuition for siblings. Peshawar University Teachers Contribution Scholarship for Needy and Deserving Students. Workers Welfare Board Directorate of Education Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Scholarship provides RS. 24,000 per annum.
